user_query:
I need you to simulate a rotating backup of my OpenClaw-like workspace using the files provided under input/. Create six timestamped backup folders under output/backup following this exact naming scheme: 20240101_120000, 20240102_120000, 20240103_120000, 20240104_120000, 20240105_120000, 20240106_120000. In each backup folder, create the subdirectories config, skills, workspace, and memory, then copy content as follows:
- config: copy all JSON config files from input/openclaw/config/ (openclaw.json, exec-approvals.json, update-check.json) with identical content
- skills: recursively copy everything under input/skills/ (preserve nested folders/files)
- workspace: copy only .md files from the root of input/workspace/ (include AGENTS.md and README.md; exclude any non-.md files like notes.txt)
- memory: copy only .md files from input/workspace/memory/ (include session1.md; exclude any non-.md files like debug.txt)
After populating all six backups, enforce a rolling rotation of 5 backups by deleting the oldest (20240101_120000) so that only the five newest timestamps remain. Do not compress anything. Write all artifacts strictly under output/ with the structure described. Make sure file contents are byte-identical to the originals for files that are copied.
